snp3.10:3.0.657: “When a person has taken birthan axe is born inside his mouthwith which the fool cuts himselfby uttering wrongful speech. (1)
snp3.10:3.0.658: “One who praises a person deserving blame,or blames a person deserving praise,collects bad luck with his mouth,by which he finds no happiness. (2)
snp3.10:3.0.659: “Slight is this bad luck:the loss of one’s wealth at dice,[the loss] of all including oneself.It is much worse bad luckto harbor hate against the holy ones.(3)
snp3.10:3.0.661: “The speaker of slander goes to hell,so too one who, having acted, says: ‘I didn’t do it.’Having passed away, both are the same,men of base actions in the next world. (5)
snp3.10:3.0.662: “When one defames an innocent man,a pure person without blemish,the evil falls back on the fool himselflike fine dust thrown against the wind.”(6)
snp3.10:3.0.663: “One intent upon the quality of greedreviles others by means of speech; [128]faithless, mean, stingy, miserly,he is intent upon divisive speech. (7)
snp3.10:3.0.664: “Foul-mouthed, liar, ignoble one,abortionist, evil one, wrongdoer,base person, unlucky man, low born:do not speak much here, you who are bound for hell. (8)
snp3.10:3.0.665: “You scatter dust to your harm,when you, evildoer, malign the good.Having done many bad deeds,you will go to the abyss for a long time. (9)
snp3.10:3.0.666: “For no one’s kamma is ever lost;since it returns, its owner obtains it.In the other world the dullard,the evildoer, sees suffering in himself. (10)
snp3.10:3.0.667: “He arrives at the place of impalement,which has iron hooks, sharp blades, and iron stakes. [129]Then there is the food, which is like a ballof hot iron, as is appropriate. (11)
snp3.10:3.0.668: “When speaking, [the wardens of hell]do not speak sweetly.They do not hasten; they do not offer shelter.Those [hell beings] lie on coals spread out;they enter a blazing mass of fire. (12)
snp3.10:3.0.669: “And capturing them in a net,there [the wardens] strike them with iron hammers;[the hell beings] come upon blinding gloom,which is extended like a mist. (13)
snp3.10:3.0.670: “Then they enter the Copper Cauldron,which is a blazing mass of fire;they are boiled in them for a long time,rising and sinking in the masses of fire. (14)
snp3.10:3.0.671: “Then the evildoer is cooked therein [copper] mixed with pus and blood. [130]Whatever region he resorts to,he is afflictedon making contact there. (15)
snp3.10:3.0.672: “There the evildoer is cookedin water that is the abode of worms.There is not even a shore to which to go,for the pots all around are all alike. (16)
snp3.10:3.0.673: “Then they enter the Wood of Sword Leaves,which is so sharp that their bodies are cut to pieces.Having grabbed the tongue with a hook,repeatedly slashing it, [the wardens] strike it. (17)
snp3.10:3.0.674: “Then they approach the impassable Vetaraṇī,with its sharp blades, with razor blades.The dullards fall into it —those evildoers who have done evil deeds. (18) [131]
snp3.10:3.0.675: “While they are weeping there,brown and spotted dogs devour them,as do flocks of ravens and very greedy jackals,while hawks and crows stab at them. (19)
snp3.10:3.0.676: “Difficult is this life here,which the evildoer sees.Therefore, in the remainder of one’s life here,a person should be dutiful and not be heedless.” (20)
snp3.10:3.0.678: As painful as the hells are said to be here,for just so long one must dwell there;therefore one should always guard speech and mindtoward those who are pure, virtuous, full of good qualities. (22)
